condition.qualitative_description: Three or more severe drought weeks in the latest kharif season, or a combination of at least one severe week and four or more moderate weeks, indicates persistent crop-season water deficit sufficient to cause yield loss.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Persistent severe drought weeks during kharif directly reduce soil moisture below crop wilting thresholds. In semi-arid Malwa and Gujarat, where baseline rainfall is 500–740 mm and hard rock aquifers offer limited buffer storage, even 3 consecutive severe weeks during critical crop growth stages (flowering, grain fill) can cause significant yield loss. The India Drought Manual uses SPI-based weekly classification to define severe drought; multiple severe weeks confirm meteorological drought as the primary water scarcity driver.

condition.qualitative_description: Four or more dry spell weeks (4-week moving window with <50% historical rainfall) in the latest kharif season indicate prolonged rainfall gaps that disrupt critical crop growth stages.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Dry spells of 4 or more weeks during kharif are particularly damaging in semi-arid zones where crops rely almost entirely on in-season rainfall. In the Malwa plateau and Gujarat plains, soybean and cotton — the dominant kharif crops — are highly sensitive to moisture stress during vegetative and reproductive stages. A 4-week dry spell window with less than 50% of historical rainfall represents a significant departure from normal that disrupts crop water demand-supply synchronisation, as documented in crop water management literature.

condition.qualitative_description: Long-run mean annual precipitation below 600 mm combined with a kharif season receiving less than 75% of the long-run annual mean confirms a below-normal monsoon year in an already moisture-limited zone.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: AER-5 (Central Malwa Highlands and Gujarat Plains) receives 500–740 mm annually, placing it at the lower end of semi-arid rainfall. When the long-run mean falls below 600 mm and the current kharif season delivers less than 75% of that mean, the zone is in a confirmed below-normal monsoon year. Seasonal precipitation breakdown is critical because rabi and zaid seasons in this zone depend on residual soil moisture and groundwater, both of which are recharged primarily during kharif. A deficit kharif season therefore cascades into multi-season water scarcity.

condition.qualitative_description: Monsoon onset in the latest year is more than 7 days later than the earliest year in the record, indicating a trend toward delayed onset that shortens the effective kharif growing season.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: Delayed monsoon onset in semi-arid zones shortens the effective kharif season, compressing the window for sowing, vegetative growth, and grain fill. In Malwa and Gujarat, where kharif crops such as soybean and groundnut have fixed phenological requirements, a delay of more than one week forces either late sowing (reducing yield potential) or dry sowing under moisture stress. Delayed onset also reduces total seasonal accumulation even if withdrawal timing is unchanged, amplifying the drought signal from sig_01 and sig_03.

condition.qualitative_description: SPI classification of moderate drought or worse combined with VCI below 35 confirms co-occurrence of meteorological and agricultural drought, ruling out irrigation-buffered scenarios.

diagnostic_signals.explanation: The India Drought Manual prescribes a multi-index approach combining SPI (meteorological drought), MAI (soil moisture adequacy), and VCI (vegetation condition) to classify drought type and severity. When SPI indicates moderate-to-extreme drought and VCI falls below 35 — the threshold associated with significant vegetation stress — the combination confirms that meteorological deficit has translated into agricultural drought affecting crop biomass. Studies on kharif rice in India show SPI and VCI are significantly correlated with productivity index, validating their joint use for drought confirmation in semi-arid cropping systems.

confounders.confounder: Groundwater over-extraction causing water scarcity independent of meteorological drought
confounders.how_to_distinguish: If drought_weeks_severe and dry_spell_weeks are low or absent in the current season but farmers still report water scarcity, the pathway is more likely groundwater stress than drought. Check trend_annual_delta_g_mm — a persistently negative trend across multiple years including wet years points to extraction-driven depletion rather than rainfall deficit. Drought-driven scarcity should co-occur with confirmed below-normal SPI and VCI signals.

confounders.confounder: Canal or tank irrigation failure (infrastructure breakdown) mimicking drought-induced water scarcity
confounders.how_to_distinguish: If seasonal_precipitation_mm for kharif is near or above the historical mean but farmers report water scarcity, investigate irrigation infrastructure status. Drought pathway requires confirmation via drought_weeks_severe >= 1 or SPI-based drought classification in drought_causality_json. Infrastructure failure would show normal or above-normal rainfall signals alongside scarcity reports, whereas drought would show consistent deficits across SPI, VCI, and dry_spell_weeks.

sources.title: Management of crop water under drought: a review
sources.relevance_note: Reviews drought stress mechanisms in crop-soil-atmosphere continuum; documents how dry spells and seasonal rainfall deficits disrupt crop water demand-supply synchronisation, supporting sig_02 and sig_03 thresholds

sources.title: Drought assessment for kharif rice using standardized precipitation index (SPI) and vegetation condition index (VCI)
sources.relevance_note: Demonstrates significant correlation between SPI and VCI with kharif crop productivity index in Indian semi-arid districts; validates joint use of SPI and VCI for agricultural drought confirmation as used in sig_05

sources.title: Drought Management Manual
authors: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
sources.relevance_note: Defines India's official multi-index drought classification framework using SPI, MAI, and VCI; specifies severe drought thresholds and dry spell definitions used in sig_01, sig_02, and sig_05

sources.title: Water–food–energy nexus with changing agricultural scenarios in India during recent decades
sources.relevance_note: Documents that post-drought groundwater storage fails to recover to pre-drought levels in Indian semi-arid regions, supporting the cascade from meteorological drought to multi-season water scarcity described in sig_03

overall_reasoning_note: Diagnosis of the drought pathway in AER-5 semi-arid hard rock terrain requires confirmation from at least two independent signals: (1) a direct drought intensity measure (sig_01: severe/moderate drought weeks, or sig_05: SPI+VCI composite), and (2) a rainfall deficit measure (sig_02: dry spell weeks, or sig_03: below-normal seasonal precipitation). Monsoon onset delay (sig_04) amplifies but does not independently confirm the pathway. A single signal may reflect data noise or spatial mismatch at block level. If all drought signals are absent but water scarcity is reported, investigate groundwater stress or irrigation infrastructure failure as alternative pathways. In hard rock aquifer settings, drought-year recharge deficits compound across seasons — a below-normal kharif translates directly into rabi and zaid water scarcity even without additional rainfall failure.
